footer.summary: Built for small technical teams that want meeting outcomes to survive the call, keep ownership explicit, and reach Notion only after a human review step.

access.dashboard: Use the dashboard to review meetings and track active sessions.
access.clerk: Connect through Clerk so the web app can hand an auth token to the extension.

hero.title: Turn Google Meet into accountable follow-through.
hero.description: Kapter records meetings, analyzes them with AI, and automatically converts them into clear tasks on Notion.

lines.speaker: Speaker A
lines.content: We can ship the review workflow this week if ownership is clear before sync starts.

lines.speaker: Speaker B
lines.content: Good. Keep a human in control, then push reviewed tasks into Notion after the meeting ends.

lines.speaker: Speaker C
lines.content: Capture decision context, not just a bullet list, so follow-through still makes sense tomorrow.

reviewGate.title: Nothing syncs without a human decision
items: Rewrite action items when the wording is off.
Map AI labels to real teammate names.
Confirm the queue before Notion becomes the source of truth.

handoff.title: From review surface to the Notion queue

items.title: Summary
items.description: Under 200 words, grounded in the real discussion flow.

items.title: Owner
items.description: Mapped to a real person before the queue leaves review.

items.title: Action item
items.description: Structured for follow-through instead of cleanup after the meeting.

workflow.title: From direct capture to confirmed task handoff.
workflow.description: The system is intentionally simple: listen during the meeting, organize afterward, then let the team approve the outcome before leaving the review surface.

steps.title: Capture the real conversation
steps.description: Start and stop from the extension while Google Meet audio streams from the active Chrome tab. No bot joins the room and no one has to talk while typing notes.

steps.title: Separate who said what
steps.description: The pipeline produces timestamped text with speaker tracks so work can stay attached to the right person instead of disappearing into anonymous notes.

steps.title: Prepare a review queue
steps.description: Kapter extracts summaries, action items, and suggested owners into a clean review surface so the team can edit quickly instead of rebuilding the outcome from scratch.

steps.title: Confirm and sync to Notion
steps.description: The team makes the final decision on content, ownership, and deadlines before any task is written to Notion. Human-in-the-loop is a product rule, not a fallback plan.

signalsSection.title: Keep the parts ordinary notes usually lose.
signalsSection.description: Kapter is designed for the messiest part of collaboration: who owns the next step, why the team chose it, and how to hand work off without creating another cleanup loop.

callout.title: Every action item keeps its meeting context.
callout.description: That means the review step can decide from evidence instead of guessing what someone meant after the call ends.

cards.title: Speaker accountability stays intact
cards.description: Normal notes flatten the conversation into vague bullet points. Kapter preserves who drove the decision so assignment review keeps enough context.

cards.title: Reasoning survives the meeting
cards.description: You keep the critical reasoning behind a task, not just the final checkbox. That makes follow-through less likely to drift later.

cards.title: Review happens before handoff
cards.description: The product is built to surface uncertainty and let the team fix it before tasks enter the system of record.

fitSection.title: One capture point. One review surface. One destination.
fitSection.description: Kapter deliberately keeps scope narrow so the workflow stays clear: Google Meet in, reviewed action-item handoff out. That focus is a product strategy, not a temporary limitation.

boundaries.label: Input
boundaries.detail: A narrow scope keeps capture predictable and prevents the product from bloating.

boundaries.label: Review surface
boundaries.detail: One place to inspect transcripts, summaries, and the action-item queue after each meeting.

boundaries.label: Final destination
boundaries.detail: The handoff ends where many small technical teams already manage work.

boundaries.label: Authentication
boundaries.detail: The web app is the trusted browser-side identity source for the extension.

extensionSetup.title: Install the Kapter extension before the official launch.
extensionSetup.description: Kapter is still in development, so extension testing currently uses Chrome developer mode instead of the Chrome Web Store.
extensionSetup.missingDownload: No direct download link is configured in this environment. Use the local build instructions on the right, or provide VITE_EXTENSION_TEST_BUILD_URL to publish a zip link for testers.
extensionSetup.afterDownload: After installing the extension, sign in to the dashboard and start recording from the popup to validate the full workflow.
stepsWithDownload: Download the latest test build zip and extract it locally.
Click Load unpacked and select the extracted extension folder.
stepsWithoutDownload: Build the extension locally (or ask the team for a build zip).
Click Load unpacked and select kapter-extension/dist (or the extracted folder).

finalCta.title: Make every meeting end with named owners and a clean handoff into Notion.
finalCta.description: Kapter keeps the nuance of the conversation, then gives the team the final say before follow-through becomes a written task.
